name: Grade 4 Language Arts — Semester 1
description: An 18-week Grade 4 pathway for structured writing, textual evidence, research, arguments, and language conventions. Learning advances through inquiry, practice, authentic application, revision, and communication.
learningGoals: Develop grade 4 knowledge and transferable skills in structured writing, textual evidence, research, arguments, and language conventions.
Use appropriate evidence, vocabulary, tools, and methods to explain reasoning.
Plan, revise, collaborate responsibly, and communicate a well-supported conclusion.

lessons.title: Launch inquiry: structured writing, textual evidence, research, arguments, and language conventions
lessons.objective: I can develop a focused question and gather relevant evidence about structured writing, textual evidence, research, arguments, and language conventions and explain my reasoning with appropriate evidence.

units.title: Investigate: structured writing, textual evidence, research, arguments, and language conventions
units.essentialQuestion: What question and evidence can guide our study of structured writing, textual evidence, research, arguments, and language conventions?
outcomes: I can develop a focused question and gather relevant evidence about structured writing, textual evidence, research, arguments, and language conventions.
I can use appropriate disciplinary vocabulary, evidence, and tools to communicate my reasoning.

activities.title: Question and evidence
activities.learnerAction: The learner develop a focused question and gather relevant evidence about structured writing, textual evidence, research, arguments, and language conventions, documents evidence in an age-appropriate format, and follows applicable safety, ethics, and collaboration expectations.

successCriteria: The learner completes a grade 4 task connected to structured writing, textual evidence, research, arguments, and language conventions.
The learner uses relevant evidence, vocabulary, or a model to explain reasoning.
The learner revises, seeks feedback, or identifies a specific next step.
